title: Global static path resolution bypasses instance-specific data_dir configuration

evidence.note: Resolves config, data, and state directories using global static helpers or a global environment variable.

evidence.quote: // TODO: gosling reads Paths::in_state_dir globally (e.g. RequestLog), ignoring this data_dir.
evidence.note: Indicates that the ACP server ignores its own data_dir option for global utilities.

observed: Log directory setups, TLS credential keys, telemetry files, and client registries are calculated globally rather than respecting GoslingAcpAgentOptions.data_dir.
expected_boundary: Every path lookup should dynamically resolve relative to the server instance context data_dir to prevent directory isolation failures.
failure_mechanism: Calculators are static and cannot accept dynamic instance variables.
break_it_angle: Start multiple ACP server instances concurrently with separate data directories; observe logs writing to the same default global location.
impact: Shared telemetry, cert collisions, and log leakage across concurrent sandboxed server processes.
adjacent: Paths calculations are used globally in configuration module initialization

mitigation.minimal_repair: Replace global static Paths lookups inside long-lived server systems with context-relative paths.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Verify directory locations during instance startup to ensure no global home folder paths are evaluated.
mitigation.behavior_test: Multiple concurrently instantiated server test fixtures with unique data_dirs produce zero state side-effects in standard home paths.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Requires refactoring paths resolution references across multiple modules and passing configuration parameters down during instantiation.

non_goals: modifying CLI strategy layout for local users
review_limits: Limited to source review; telemetry installation checks were not ran.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Paths helper functions do not accept runtime variables and explicitly fallback to strategies.
